title: The Brew Shop - Weekly Email Update
contentHtml:
View this email in your browser

The Brew Shop Weekly Update

Hey Brew Shop Fam!
Here's an update of what's happening this week - from new beer drops, wine reviews, our current tap list, to upcoming tastings, and more. 

SHOP THIS WEEK’S NEWSLETTER HERE
SHOP OUR ENTIRE STOCK OF BEER, WINE & HOMEBREW HERE
 
Spring Wine Fest!

Wine Fest on a weeknight?! Why, yes! Join us for The Brew Shop's Annual Spring Wine Festival on Thursday, May 14 from 5:30 - 8:30 pm!

- Walk-about tasting featuring 20 different Spring-forward wines

- Outdoors and indoors! Enjoy the Spring weather with outdoor tasting stations along our front sidewalk

- Featuring artisanal cheese and crackers

- Discounts for retail purchases on all wines

- Chat, learn and sip wine with our wonderful team of in-the-know wine distributors

- A great way to stock up for Spring outings, family gatherings and patio time

To give us a little more space, the tasting has been split into two sessions: 5:30-7 pm and 7-8:30 pm. Tickets are $20 per person ($15 for Wine Club Members). Please choose the time slot that works best for you!

Wine Club Member? Remember to put in your special code at check out for 10% off!

PURCHASE SPRING WINE FEST TICKETS HERE
 
Friday Free Tasting!

Join us this Friday from 5-7 PM as we welcome Adam from Aslin for our free tasting! Adam will be pouring a lineup of both new and classic Aslin brews. Scroll down to see our tasting line-up for May.

New wines in the door:

Bodega Morenas Gran Buque Cava: This bottle is exactly why Cava is our favorite go-to, affordable sparkler. Scroll down to read Sam’s write-up!

Vina Almirante Vanidade Albarino: Find yourself some spare change, because the little circles on the front of this bottle are scratchers! Hailing from the prestigious Val do Salnés subzone of Rías Baixas, the grapes are partially cryomacerated - aka frozen! - for greater depth and flavor before they undergo stainless steel fermentation. In the glass, expect seaside vibes of white peach, fresh citrus, and a mineral salinity that whisks you straight to the Atlantic coast. Hand harvested.

Domaine St. Laurent Rose: Coming from Pinot Noir grown at a striking 1,625-foot elevation on volcanic soils, Domaine St. Laurent is held on the skins for just four hours to achieve its signature delicate pink hue before fermenting in stainless steel to preserve freshness and crisp acidity. Aromas of white peach and delicate florals with strawberries and stone fruit underpinned by a lovely terroir-driven (volcanic!) minerality. Hand Harvested.

Bloodroot Cabernet Sauvignon: Bloodroot is a collaboration among some of California's most celebrated winemakers — think Banshee and Rickshaw — who have joined forces to craft a bold, brooding red that lives up to its dramatic name. Aged in a combo of new, used and neutral oak, Bloodroot delivers with dark plum and blackberry paired with whispers of baking spice, toast through the middle and gorgeous dry finish.

What we’re drinking this week:

Gran Buque Cava is basically what’s getting me through these last few weeks of the school year. I’m officially in countdown mode, and this is exactly what I picture popping open the second summer starts.  

Here's what our shop has to say about it -

"Old-world craft meets effervescent joy. Toasted brioche and green apple lead the way, layered with hints of citrus zest and a chalky mineral depth that speaks of the Penedès terroir. This bottle is exactly why Cava is our favorite go-to, affordable sparkler."

Cava, for anyone who doesn’t already have a go-to, is Spain’s take on high-quality traditional-method sparkling wine. The subtle bready notes come from the wine resting with yeast in the bottle during its second fermentation, which adds a little extra richness and depth to the bubbles. It comes mainly from the Penedès region, just outside Barcelona, where you get that perfect balance of sunshine and freshness that really shows up in the glass.  

It’s crisp, refreshing, and just feels like summer in a glass. Easy to drink, easy to pair (mussels, fish, or something salty), and even easier to enjoy when you’ve got nowhere to be the next day.  

This week’s writeup is brought to you by Sam S.

May Tasting Events are spring ready!

 

Thanks for your business! See you soon, Beth, Julie & Aidan and the entire Brew Crew
